topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The plastic stick felt heavy in my hand, the results staring back at me with an unwelcome clarity. I stared at the screen, my jaw tightening until my teeth ached. It was done. After years of carefully constructed indifference, this revelation felt like a physical blow. My hands, usually restless, were suddenly stiff, clenched into fists against the worn fabric of the armchair. "No," I muttered, the sound rough and low, barely audible over the hum of the refrigerator. I pushed the device across the cluttered coffee table, the movement sharp and impatient. The air in the small living room seemed to thicken, suffocating. I didn't want to process this; I just wanted it to stop existing.

[story 2]

I kept rubbing my temples, a dull throb behind my eyes mirroring the churning in my stomach. The report lay spread across the kitchen counter, the colors of the genetic markers mocking my attempts to maintain composure. Every line of text seemed to vibrate with an unwelcome truth. I leaned back against the counter, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, a defensive posture against the sudden intrusion. "This is absurd," I snapped at the empty room, my voice clipped and sharp. My gaze darted around the apartment, cataloging every imperfection, searching for an escape route, a way to make the information simply vanish. I picked up a glass of water, but the liquid felt cold and unsatisfying.

[story 3]

The sheer volume of data on the screen was overwhelming, a cascade of undeniable facts hitting me all at once. I let out a slow, deliberate exhale, the sound laced with profound annoyance. I dropped the testing kit onto the floor with a decisive thud. My shoulders slumped, a heavy weight settling onto my spine. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small perimeter of the room, my footsteps echoing unevenly. "What now?" I asked the silence, the tone laced with a deep-seated weariness. I ran a hand through my hair, pulling at the strands as if trying to physically undo the implications of what I had just discovered.
